data.content: Needs implicit usings — let me fix:

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/stackalloc_test && sed -i 's|</PropertyGroup>|<ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>\\n  </PropertyGroup>|' stackalloc_test.csproj && dotnet run 2>&1", "description": "Enable implicit usings and rerun", "initial_wait": 30}

data.content:
**Yes, C# fully supports `stackalloc`'d `Span<nint>` and `Span<nuint>`.** Both compiled and ran without issues on .NET 8:

```
nint:  a[0]=42, a[1]=9223372036854775807, length=4
nuint: b[0]=99, b[1]=18446744073709551615, length=4
```

`nint.MaxValue` resolved to `long.MaxValue` (2⁶³−1) and `nuint.MaxValue` to `ulong.MaxValue` (2⁶⁴−1), confirming this ran on a 64-bit platform where the native integer size is 8 bytes. The syntax is straightforward:

```csharp
Span<nint>  a = stackalloc nint[4];
Span<nuint> b = stackalloc nuint[4];
```

This has been supported since C# 9 / .NET 5, when `nint`/`nuint` were introduced as first-class types (aliases for `System.IntPtr`/`System.UIntPtr`).
